other_observers: Jeannie and Ron Mitchell, Brandon Percival, John Drummond, Dan Maynard; Steve Brown and Brian Gibbons; first identified by Brian Gibbons as he took a bird out of a bag for banding.
duration: on-and-off for 15 minutes or so, some of it in the hand possing for photos
specific_location: RMBO Banding Station, Chico Basin Ranch
description: This bird was easily separated as one of the three look-alike Oporornis Warblers by its gray hood, yellow underparts and olive-green back. Specifically, this bird had a very long bill that was dusky on top and pink of the "lower mandible". The legs were dull pink. The light gray face gradually became greenish distal to the bird's nape. The white eye ring was very noticeable. (I saw the bird in the hand for about a minute for close examination). The yellow undertail coverts were examined and they almost reached the end of the bird's tail.Behaviors: I refound the bird about an hour after it had been banded and released. It was seen over 5 minutes moving sluggishly from 3-7 feet off the ground, not really feeding, but moving to stay in cover, or so it appeared. At one time it walked a few steps on a horizontal branch. I also saw it fly (a second time) into a msit net. The bird's behavior then changed dramatically.Call: non heardPlumage: alternate
